results.title: Louis Sepe
highlights:
As a technology leader in Fortune 500 environments, I equip businesses with the insights and capabilities to evolve for the future and find new ways to compete and engage. Much of my recent career has focused on business and technology transformations. I have initiated innovation agendas and led strategic initiatives spanning merchandising, eCommerce, marketing technology, data and analytics, business intelligence and systems impacting every aspect of the corporation. In my last role as Chief Data Officer / VP of Information Technology at Bed Bath & Beyond, Inc., I combined enterprise technologies with an understanding of business requirements to strengthen the company’s response to shifting forces. I headed our enterprise data warehouse, marketing technology, demand & supply planning systems and enterprise quality assurance. In addition I co-led our corporate IT architectural review board. Previous executive IT positions focused on investment projects that upgraded our ability to attract more profitable customers at lower operating costs. I created new departments focused on analysis and optimization, resulting in business decisions underpinned by reliable data and an understanding of trends. From a people perspective, I’m known for collaboration and innovation and for attracting high-caliber talent to my teams. My approach has fostered deep change in business and operating models and enabled strategic re-positioning that gave new life to business legacies.
...

...

...
Job Category: Data AI & ML Jobs

### Responsibilities
...
- Develop and apply machine learning models, including techniques such as decision trees and forecasting, to solve business problems
- Use generative artificial intelligence methods to extract insights from complex document sets, including structured and unstructured text
- Perform data analysis, feature engineering, and model development using Python
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to interpret data, communicate findings, and support decision-making
- Translate domain-specific language and requirements into actionable data science solutions
- Continuously refine models and analytical approaches to improve accuracy and performance
...
### Required experience and skills
...
- Strong background in data science, with hands-on experience in machine learning model development
- Proficiency in Python, including libraries such as Pandas for data manipulation and analysis
- Working knowledge of SQL for querying and managing data
- Experience with generative artificial intelligence, including prompt-based techniques
- Solid understanding of statistical methods and analytical techniques
- Experience with machine learning approaches such as tree-based models and forecasting methods
- Ability to communicate complex analytical concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders

results.title: AI Data Scientist | Jobflarely
highlights:
AI Data Scientist | Jobflarely
...
# AI Data Scientist
...
Burlington Stores, Inc. is a growing company committed to being an employer of choice. They are seeking a Junior AI Data Scientist to support various business areas by analyzing data and developing AI-driven solutions to enhance retail operations and customer experiences.
...
- Work with stakeholders throughout the organization including Merchandising, Allocations, Marketing, IT and Supply Chain Analytics to assist in developing and implementing AI-powered solutions
- Mine and analyze structured and unstructured data from the data warehouse
- Support prompt engineering and optimization for large language models (LLMs)
- Integrate AI services via APIs and cloud AI platforms
- Keep up to date with latest technology trends
- Work closely with senior data scientists to expand technical and functional knowledge as well as apply best practices
- Coordinate with different functional teams to identify AI automation opportunities
- Develop processes and tools to monitor and analyze model performance and data accuracy
- Translate complex analytical findings into clear business recommendations
...
- Bachelor's degree in AI, Data Science, Computer Science, or related field
- 0–2 years of experience in AI, data analytics, or related technical role
- Knowledge in Large Language Models (LLMs), Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), and AI model evaluation concepts
- Exposure to cloud AI platforms (Azure AI, AWS AI services, or GCP Vertex AI)
- 2+ years Data Science experience with extensive background in building ML models, data mining and statistical analysis
- Strong interpersonal and relationship building skills
- Strong written and verbal communication skills

highlights:
Senior AI Scientist | Hirequorum

# Senior AI Scientist
...
As the Senior AI Scientist, you will work alongside industry experts developing and applying cutting edge ML & AI algorithms to optimize Best Buy customer experience. The systems you build will power customer experiences responsible for billions of dollars in revenues from home page, search, recommendations to checkout and post-purchase. You will work in a dynamic team tackling a variety of use cases including understanding search intent, intelligently optimizing the page layout, deciding what products to be shown in carousels, trading off between giving customers what they want vs allowing for serendipity, etc. You will work alongside a fast-paced team enabling ideas to often go into production in a matter of weeks.
...
### What you’ll do
...
- Design and implement large-scale Search, RecSys, Personalization and Adtech systems that power customer experiences across Best Buy surfaces.
- Derive complex features from large scale clickstream logs for model training data
- Develop & finetune retrieval/ranking models using supervised learning, deep learning, LLMs, and generative AI (e.g., Two Tower models, Sequence models, GNNs, etc.)
- Design custom evaluation metrics for measuring quality of ML and GenAI models.
- Collaborate with AI engineers, platform and product teams to deploy low latency models.
...
### Basic qualifications
...
- Bachelor's degree in a highly quantitative field (Computer Science, Engineering, Physics, Math, Operations Research or related) or equivalent experience
- Strong machine learning and algorithmic background with a deep understanding of supervised and unsupervised learning methods, deep learning, LLMs.
- 4 years of experience building ML and/or AI driven products or other similar related functions (e.g. software engineering, data science, advanced analytics). Advanced degrees in relevant fields may be counted towards experience requirements.
- 2 years experience working on industrial-grade Search, RecSys, Personalization or Adtech.
- Strong skills in SQL and at least 1 programming language (e.g., Python, Julia)
...
### Preferred qualifications
...
- Master's degree or Ph.D in a highly quantitative field (Computer Science, Engineering, Physics, Math, Operations Research or related)
- Experience building production grade ML models using one or more Deep Learning frameworks like TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch, etc.
- Cross-functional experience in deploying realtime low-latency ML products over GCP or AWS
...

results.title: [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II | Wfh
highlights:
# [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II
...
Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Cotiviti is a healthcare technology company focused on delivering better care at lower costs through advanced technology and data analytics. The Senior Generative AI Scientist II will work with a team to solve healthcare issues using machine learning and AI techniques, delivering value through innovative data solutions. Responsibilities • Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Business Operations, along with Product Management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques • Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases • Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work • Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA • Work with ML Ops/Production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance • Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform • Participate in other data science initiatives, collaborating with your peers to support their projects • Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team • Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption • Complete all responsibilities as outlined in the annual performance review and/or goal setting • Complete all special projects and other duties as assigned • Must be able to perform duties with or without reasonable accommodation Skills • Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI • Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases • 5+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU • Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP • Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets • Experience communicating technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences is a plus • Flexibility to work with global teams as well geographically dispersed US based teams • Professional with ability to properly handle confidential information • Ability to work within a matrixed organization • Proficiency in all required skills and competencies above Benefits • Medical • Dental • Vision • Disability • Life insurance coverage • 401(k) savings plans • Paid family leave • 9 paid holidays per year • 17-27 days of Paid Time Off (PTO) per year, depending on specific level and length of service with Cotiviti. Company Overview • Cotiviti enables healthcare organizations to deliver better care at lower cost through advanced technology and data analytics that improve the quality and sustainability of healthcare in the United States. It was founded in 1979, and is headquartered in South Jordan, UT, US, with a workforce of 5001-10000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship • Cotiviti has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 165 in 2025, 118 in 2024, 90 in 2023, 102 in 2022, 72 in 2021, 67 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

Google's software engineers develop the next-generation technologies that change how billions of users connect, explore, and interact with information and one another. Our products need to handle information at massive scale, and extend well beyond web search. We're looking for engineers who bring fresh ideas from all areas, including information retrieval, distributed computing, large-scale system design, networking and data storage, security, artificial intelligence, natural language processing, UI design and mobile; the list goes on and is growing every day. As a software engineer, you will work on a specific project critical to Google’s needs with opportunities to switch teams and projects as you and our fast-paced business grow and evolve. We need our engineers to be versatile, display leadership qualities and be enthusiastic to take on new problems across the full-stack as we continue to push technology forward.

results.title: Senior Data Scientist, Platform | Wfhforgeon
highlights:
Senior Data Scientist, Platform | Wfhforgeon
...
# Senior Data Scientist, Platform
...
Trust is the foundation of a vibrant Airbnb community. The Content Integrity Data Science team helps build and protect that trust by ensuring that the content on Airbnb, including listings, profiles, messages and other user-generated experiences, is accurate, authentic and aligned with our policies and community standards.
...
We partner closely with product, engineering, policy and operations teams to develop advanced AI-driven content understanding systems, scale effective human-in-the-loop workflows and proactively defend against emerging content risks, so guests and hosts can interact with confidence.
...
This role is unique in that it directly improves the safety, trust, and quality of real-world user experiences by advancing Airbnb’s ability to understand, interpret and act on content at scale. You will help shape how the platform reasons about listings, profiles, messages and other user-generated content by building the next generation of Trust Content Understanding Models.
...
- The ideal candidate is a motivated and talented “full-stack” Data Scientist with strong applied ML intuition and a bias toward impact, who can own and drive forward challenging, high-visibility initiatives such as:
...
- Advance Airbnb’s content integrity capabilities by building Natural Language Processing (NLP) and LLM-based models that understand intent, policy compliance, quality and risk across listings, profiles, and user communications
...
- Develop high-performing models for detecting problematic or misleading content, including text classification, semantic similarity, information extraction and generative model-based reasoning for policy interpretation and enforcement
...
- Design and optimize human-in-the-loop Machine Learning (ML) systems for content review, labeling, escalation and continuous model improvement
...
- Build systems to detect emerging content risks and abuse patterns across regions, cohorts and surfaces using statistical, ML and representation-learning approaches
...
- Design intelligent sampling and evaluation strategies to measure rare events, policy recall, false positives/negatives and model blind spots in large-scale content systems
...
- A Typical Day:
...
- Artificial Intelligence / Machine Learning: Build and deploy production AI/ML systems for content integrity and trust content understanding, including feature engineering, model development and evaluation, thresholding, error analysis and end-to-end model lifecycle management. This includes working with NLP and LLM-based models in real production settings.
...
- Inference: Partner with inference data scientists to conduct rigorous quantitative analyses, applying working knowledge of causal inference to interpret results, assess impact, and identify gaps and opportunities to improve content quality and trust outcomes.
...
- Empowerment: Think strategically about how to scale and evolve Airbnb’s content integrity defenses, helping define the long-term vision for the role of AI-driven content understanding across the Trust ecosystem.
...
- Your Expertise:
...
- 5+ years of industry experience in a quantitative analysis role with a Master’s degree in a quantitative field (computer science, statistics etc.), or 2+ years of experience with a Ph.D.
- State-of-the-art knowledge of AI/ML models
- Hands-on experience building, evaluating, and deploying NLP and LLM-based solutions, including text classification, information extraction, semantic understanding or generative applications.
...
- Working knowledge of causal inference
...
- Skilled in statistical programming (Python or R) and database usage (SQL)

results.title: Nickolas G.
highlights:
Principal Data Scientist | Causal Inference & Machine Learning for Strategic Decision-Making | Systems Thinker & Mentor
...
Lead Data Scientist at [Best Buy](https://www.linkedin.com/company/best-buy)
...
As a Lead Data Scientist at Best Buy, I bridge advanced analytics and real-world impact. My work focuses on causal inference, machine learning, and econometrics to guide high-stakes decisions in areas like demand forecasting, marketing strategy, and supply chain resilience. I thrive at the intersection of technical depth and strategic relevance, designing modular, interpretable models that capture the nuances of consumer behavior, market dynamics, and organizational complexity. Whether I’m building long-horizon forecasts or experimentation pipelines, my goal is to enable data-driven strategies that are not only efficient, but resilient—especially in turbulent economic and policy environments. My foundation in physics, mathematics, and computer science is paired with a systems-thinking mindset, allowing me to synthesize insights across domains—from game theory to social choice—to support smarter collective decisions. I’m especially passionate about: - Designing causal modeling frameworks that go beyond surface-level correlations - Driving cross-functional alignment between data science and strategic planning - Mentoring the next generation of data scientists with empathy and intellectual rigor I'm especially interested in connecting with people who are thinking deeply about how to bring analytical rigor into real-world complexity—those who are shaping strategy, growing resilient teams, or advancing the role of data science in organizational decision-making. Whether through collaboration, conversation, or shared curiosity, I’m always looking to learn from others who are working at the intersection of analytics, leadership, and long-term impact.
...
